symptoms.symptom_name: Severe colicky RUQ/epigastric pain radiating to the right subscapular region

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postprandial colicky RUQ pain radiating to the right shoulder blade is the classic biliary-colic pattern.
Same site and trigger; cholecystitis is the persistent, inflamed continuation of biliary obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset shortly after a high-fat meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatty food triggers cholecystokinin-driven gallbladder contraction against an obstructing stone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked restlessness — pacing, unable to stay still

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visceral colicky pain characteristically drives movement, unlike peritonitis where patients lie still.
Peritonitic patients keep still; restlessness argues against established peritonism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe nausea with one episode of non-bloody vomiting; unable to tolerate oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nausea and vomiting commonly accompany biliary colic.
Intractable vomiting can accompany pancreatitis; non-specific but supports the need for lipase.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior similar self-limiting episodes (resolved in 3-4 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent stereotyped self-limiting postprandial attacks are typical of symptomatic gallstones.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever / rigors reported (patient-reportable pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors lowers cholangitis probability but does not exclude early/anicteric disease; a measured temperature is still required.
Fever may be absent early in cholecystitis; a negative self-report is weak evidence.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visible jaundice on video; no dark urine/pale stool reported (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No jaundice makes common-duct obstruction less likely, but video assessment is unreliable and LFTs are still required to exclude it.
Tachycardia here reflects severe pain/distress; it is non-specific but would also be seen with sepsis or hypovolaemia, hence needs in-person vitals.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative Murphy's sign; focal RUQ tenderness only; no guarding/rigidity/rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative Murphy's sign lowers cholecystitis likelihood — but it is a palpation finding the telehealth AI cannot obtain.
A soft, non-rigid abdomen argues strongly against perforation/peritonitis — again, a clinician exam finding.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Transabdominal ultrasound of the right upper quadrant
investigation_details.expected_result: Mobile echogenic foci with posterior acoustic shadowing (gallstones); assess wall thickness, pericholecystic fluid and common bile duct calibre
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Gallstones and complications (cholecystitis, duct dilatation) go unconfirmed; diagnosis remains presumptive.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ultrasound demonstration of gallstones confirms cholelithiasis as the substrate; it is pending outpatient in this case, not available in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oral paracetamol ineffective for the pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biliary colic is typically poorly responsive to simple oral analgesia and often needs an NSAID; supports severity and the need for in-person analgesia.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Postprandial severe colicky RUQ pain radiating to the right shoulder, triggered by fat, with prior self-limiting attacks — strongly indicates symptomatic gallstones.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Cholangitis red-flag cluster (Charcot's triad) — screened negative
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fever + RUQ pain + jaundice defines cholangitis; the patient-reportable elements are negative here, but the cluster must be actively screened and safety-netted because it is the lethal complication.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical examination (abdominal palpation, Murphy's sign, temperature, vitals)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single highest-yield next step the AI cannot perform; distinguishes uncomplicated colic from cholecystitis/peritonism and provides objective vitals.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBC, CRP, LFTs, serum lipase
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for cholecystitis (inflammatory markers), choledocholithiasis/cholangitis (cholestatic LFTs) and gallstone pancreatitis (lipase); none obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Transabdominal ultrasound of the right upper quadrant
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms gallstones and assesses for wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid and duct dilatation; same-day if complications are suspected, otherwise early outpatient.
Prudent to exclude an inferior MI presenting as upper-abdominal pain, particularly given cardiovascular risk factors; the source note explicitly considered MI.
Not a first-line telehealth-directed request for a stable biliary-colic pattern; imaging choice belongs to the in-person assessor and ultrasound is first-line.
